other_observers: Chris Knight was with me, when we found it.  Ed Landi was able to see and record it about 30-45 mintues later
duration: 10 minutes trying to get photos and recordings
description: Chris Knight and I were birding at Valco Ponds #4 and #5, when we first heard a wren, giving a chip similar to Wilson's Warbler.  We got looks at it, and it seemed right for a Pacific Wren, rather than Winter Wren.  The throat was dark, not light, the eyeline was more buffy, not white.  The spots in the wings seemed more buffy then white.  Clearly a Winter or Pacific Wren, a short tailed wren.  Ed Landi's recording can be heard here: https://macaulaylibrary.org/asset/410200651  Since the e-bird reviewers confirmed this sighting, I decided to document this sighting for the CBRC.  I know, at least one of my previous possible Pacific Wrens wasn't accepted, since the photos weren't great.  So, I wasn't sure I should document this sighting or not.  See my photos of this bird, hopefully they show what you want to see, for a Pacific Wren, and recording is good enough as well.
photographer: Photographer: Brandon K Percival, Brandon K Percival, Brandon K Percival
